tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Brain Implant Restores Voice: Paralyzed Man Speaks Again After Nearly Two Years of Silent Independence
stories.summary: Casey Harrell regained his voice through a brain implant, communicating independently from home for two years without daily supervision.

stories.headline: Vozinha's Impossible Dream: 40-Year-Old Out-of-Contract Goalkeeper Stops World Cup Giant Spain
stories.summary: An unheralded 40-year-old goalkeeper without a team scripted a fairy tale, keeping a clean sheet as tiny Cape Verde drew against World Cup favorites.

stories.headline: First Gene Therapy for Epilepsy: Eight-Month-Old Receives Breakthrough Treatment That Stops Seizures
stories.summary: An eight-month-old infant became the world's first patient to receive gene therapy restoring the WWOX gene, halting severe seizures.

stories.headline: Cabo Verde's Turtle Triumph: Loggerhead Nesting Soars 80-Fold Through Dedicated Conservation
stories.summary: Night patrol teams on Boa Vista now encounter 30-40 loggerhead turtles nightly, up from just 5-10, marking an 80-fold increase over 27 years.

stories.headline: Neighbors Rally: Children's Lemonade Stand Raises Over $2,000 for Family Who Lost Home to Fire
stories.summary: Neighborhood kids raised $2,000 at a lemonade stand for a family who lost everything in a fire, with community support reaching nearly $150,000.

stories.headline: Neighbors Rescue Elderly Woman From Burning Home: Mount Vernon Park Community in Action
stories.summary: Neighbors banded together to pull an elderly woman from her burning house, embodying their neighborhood motto of 'Neighbors helping neighbors.'

stories.headline: One Mom's Facebook Post Becomes a Flood of Food: Hobe Sound Community Fills Little Free Pantry
stories.summary: Jacquelyn Koester's Little Free Pantry behind Hobe Sound Public Library emptied so quickly that a neighbor drove up from Jupiter, saying it would help get them through the week.

stories.headline: Coral Restoration Reaches 10,000: Waianae Coast Reefs Revived After Six Years of Dedicated Work
stories.summary: Kuleana Coral Restoration reached 10,000 corals revived on Oahu's Waianae Coast after six years, with plans to install two coral nursery classrooms this summer.

stories.headline: New Zealand's Rising Star: 26-Year-Old Eli Just Scores Twice in World Cup Debut, Lifts All Whites
stories.summary: A young striker brought joy to underdog New Zealand with two goals in his World Cup debut, securing a valuable point against higher-ranked Iran.

stories.headline: Australian Outback Comes Alive: Beloved Burrowing Bettongs Return After Decades of Extinction
stories.summary: Burrowing bettongs, football-sized relatives of kangaroos, were released in outback NSW after a decade-long rewilding project brought them back from local extinction.

stories.headline: Parents Beam With Pride: Cape Verdean Defender's Family Celebrates Historic World Cup Heroics
stories.summary: A Cape Verdean defender's Irish-Cape Verdean parents witnessed their son play a vital role in his nation's stunning World Cup upset.

stories.headline: Historic Dam Falls: 750 'Functionally Extinct' Mussels Return to Virginia's River
stories.summary: More than 750 alewife floater mussels returned to Virginia's South Anna River just two years after the 200-year-old Ashland Mill Dam was removed.

stories.headline: Kindness Rocks Hidden Across Village: Glen Carbon Seniors Spread Joy at 100th Homecoming
stories.summary: Glen Carbon Senior Center members painted decorated rocks for children to find at Miner Park and Schon Park during the village's 100th Homecoming celebration.

stories.headline: 79 Green Sea Turtle Hatchlings Safely Reach the Sea in Panglao, Bohol
stories.summary: Seventy-nine endangered green sea turtle hatchlings safely reached the ocean in Bohol thanks to dedicated protection and careful monitoring efforts.

stories.headline: Swiss Scientists Print Living Human Ear: 3D Bioprinting Reaches Life-Size Breakthrough
stories.summary: Using 70 times more efficient technology, scientists printed a life-size human ear with living cells inside, opening doors to real-scale tissue implants.

stories.headline: Shabby Bus Stop Becomes Charming Community Library: Kent Pub Team Creates Neighborhood Gem
stories.summary: The Woolpack Inn team in Chilham, Kent transformed a shabby bus shelter into a community library with a chandelier and lending books, receiving an anonymous £100 donation.
